title: erica tetralix f. rubra
intro: Erica tetralix f. rubra, also known as the red cross-leaved heath, is a subspecies of Erica tetralix, a type of flowering plant in the heath family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The red cross-leaved heath is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Erica tetralix f. rubra can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat conditions like bronchitis and coughs.
